nativeRange.habitat: Java Fern Windelov is a nursery cultivar developed from Microsorum pteropus, whose parent species originates from Southeast Asian rainforest streams, riverbanks, and waterfalls, where it grows epiphytically on submerged or emergent rocks and wood.

summary: Java Fern Windelov is a patented cultivar of Java Fern distinguished by its elegantly branched, lacy leaf tips. It shares the same undemanding epiphytic care as the parent species — kept attached to wood or rock with its rhizome above the substrate — and stays compact at 10 to 20 cm tall.
careNotes: Java Fern Windelov (Microsorum pteropus 'Windelov', also spelled 'Windeløv') is a patented cultivar selected and popularized by the Danish nursery Tropica in the 1980s, named after Tropica's founder Holger Windeløv. It shares identical care requirements with the standard Java Fern but is visually distinct: each leaf tip subdivides into elegant, finely branched, lacy fingers rather than terminating in a smooth flat blade. Plants stay noticeably smaller than the type form — typically 10–20 cm tall — and grow more slowly, making Windelov a strong choice for foreground or midground accents on hardscape where the standard Java Fern would overgrow. CRITICAL husbandry rule (shared with all Java Fern variants): the horizontal rhizome (the thick, creeping stem from which leaves and roots emerge) MUST stay above the substrate. Burying it causes rot and plant death. Attach the plant to driftwood, porous rock, or other hardscape with thread, fishing line, or cyanoacrylate gel until roots anchor naturally (typically several weeks). As an epiphyte (a plant that anchors to surfaces rather than soil), it absorbs nutrients primarily through its leaves and rhizome from the water column, so liquid fertilization — including potassium supplementation as the plant grows — is generally more useful than substrate dosing. Excessive light encourages algae growth on the slow-growing fronds. CO2 is not required, though supplementation modestly speeds growth. Like the parent species, Windelov is not eaten by most herbivorous fish, so it pairs well with goldfish and African cichlid setups that would shred softer plants, and it even tolerates mildly brackish water. Small dark spots on leaf undersides are sporangia (reproductive structures), not disease. Several other Java Fern cultivars exist in the hobby, including Trident, Narrow Leaf, Needle Leaf, and Philippine varieties.
breedingNotes: Java Fern Windelov spreads through the same two methods as the parent species. The first is rhizome division: cut a section of healthy rhizome with several leaves and roots and re-attach it to new hardscape. The second is adventitious plantlets: mature leaves spontaneously produce small daughter plants along their edges and tips (which on Windelov are themselves lacy), and these can be detached once they have a few leaves and trailing roots and then attached to new hardscape. Plantlet production accelerates noticeably on stressed or dying leaves. True spore reproduction is possible from emersed fronds but rarely occurs in submerged aquarium conditions. Windelov is a patented cultivar, so commercial propagation may be restricted in some jurisdictions, though private hobbyist propagation is universally practiced.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Primary source for cultivar origin (patented variety named after Tropica founder Holger Windeløv), light demand (low), CO2 demand (low), growth rate (slow), height (10–20+ cm after two months), placement (stone/tree root, rhizome must not be covered), care difficulty (easy), and that it is not eaten by herbivorous fish.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Used for temperature range (68–82 °F ≈ 20–28 °C), low-to-moderate light, CO2 not required (beneficial), foreground-to-midground placement on hardscape, slow-to-medium growth rate, easy care level, and the lacy/finely branched leaf-tip description distinguishing Windelov from the type form.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked beginner care level, low light tolerance, plantlet propagation, attachment to rock/driftwood with super glue, tolerance of goldfish/African-cichlid/mildly-brackish tanks, and benefit from potassium supplementation as the plant grows.

additional.name: AquaMate parent entry: Java Fern (fw-plant-019)
additional.notes: Inherited water-parameter tolerance ranges (pH 5.5–7.5, gH 2–15, kH 0–14), epiphyte/rhizome care rules, family/order, and native-range data from the parent species entry. Windelov is a cultivar of Microsorum pteropus, so parent-species values apply except where Windelov-specific sources differ (notably the smaller 10–20 cm height).
